raw.fir_template:
Subject: Complaint regarding suspicious APK file and attempted cyber fraud.

To, The Nodal Officer, NCRP. 

I am reporting a suspicious incident involving a malicious APK file (App Package). On 2026-04-07, I encountered a link/file which appears to be a tool for unauthorized access to my device. The technical details involve a connection to api.example.com. Although no financial loss has occurred yet, this appears to be a precursor to KYC or Impersonation fraud. I have not shared any OTPs or installed the software. I am filing this complaint to bring the source of this malicious file to your notice for technical investigation and to prevent potential financial theft from my bank accounts. 

Details:
- Incident Date: 2026-04-07
- Domain involved: api.example.com
- Risk: High (Malware/Spyware delivery)

I request you to block the associated URLs and take necessary action against the perpetrators.

calendar_event.title: Security Audit & Malware Cleanup - Case SATARK-30A5E463
calendar_event.description: Follow up on device security after APK threat. Verify no unauthorized access. Report to https://cybercrime.gov.in

raw.plain_english_summary: The analyzed APK appears to be a legitimate, simple notes application. It requests only basic internet access and connects to a standard API endpoint. While the strings 'otp' and 'credential' were found, the context within the code (e.g., 'no otp no credential capture') suggests these are descriptive strings confirming the absence of such features rather than functional code for stealing data. No dangerous permissions like SMS reading or audio recording are present.
